purpose: Implement carry-forward #1 from parent cycle: --cross-publish flag on tool/signals_sync.hexa for the 20 only-in-nexus CROSS_PUBLISH ids. Flag is env-gated by NEXUS_SIGNALS_SYNC_PUBLISH=1 AND --direction <nexus-to-n6arch|n6arch-to-nexus> AND --confirm-direction-<dir> matching --direction (non-interactive sign-off). Composes with --emit-patch to write review-only unified-diff patches to design/patches/signals/cross_publish/ without env-gate. Also implements --batch sig1,sig2,... orthogonal scope filter for both --apply-trivial and --cross-publish pipelines. THIS KICK BUILDS THE TOOLING; IT DOES NOT MUTATE EITHER /Users/ghost/core/nexus/n6/atlas.signals.n6 OR /Users/ghost/core/canon/atlas/atlas.signals.n6.

tier_1_implemented.rationale: Added --cross-publish flag with TRIPLE-LAYERED gate at main() entry: (1) --direction must equal 'nexus-to-n6arch' or 'n6arch-to-nexus' or refuse with raw 66 reason=cross-publish-without-direction; (2) when mutation_intent (no --emit-patch composition OR env=1), env_var('NEXUS_SIGNALS_SYNC_PUBLISH') must equal exact string '1' or refuse with reason=cross-publish-without-env-gate; (3) the matching --confirm-direction-<dir> flag must be present (non-interactive sign-off — refuses to silently mutate cross-repo without explicit per-direction confirmation) or refuse with reason=cross-publish-without-direction-confirm. Cross-publish pipeline additionally re-verifies env-gate per-action defensively. Patch-only review path (--cross-publish --emit-patch with no env) is permitted as it produces zero mutation; downgrades do_publish=false at pipeline call site. NO backdoor, NO default-on, NO override flag. Mutation requires the full ceremony: env=1 AND --direction=X AND --confirm-direction-X.

Composed --cross-publish with --emit-patch via separate output directory (design/patches/signals/cross_publish/) to keep CROSS_PUBLISH audit trail distinct from APPLY_TRIVIAL trail. Patch format = unified diff between empty file and the source's @S block (semantically: 'append this block to target'). Header --label tags both sides with target_label:atlas.signals.n6:<sig_id> and the (after-publish, sourced-from <src_label>) annotation makes the publication direction unambiguous in the .patch text. Added --batch sig1,sig2,... ORTHOGONAL scope filter that gates BOTH --apply-trivial and --cross-publish: out-of-batch ids are silently [SKIP-BATCH]'d (counted in skipped totals). Empty batch = all-in-class candidates (default behavior preserved). Cross-publish patch-only mode (--cross-publish --emit-patch, no env) is the standard review pathway; cross-publish + env + confirm + (optional --emit-patch) is the authorized-mutation path.

falsifiers.name: cross-publish-flag-recognized
falsifiers.criterion: --cross-publish parses without 'unknown arg' error; coexists with --direction, --confirm-direction-<dir>, --emit-patch, --batch; triggers respective code paths
falsifiers.evidence: Smoke test: hexa run tool/signals_sync.hexa --cross-publish --direction n6arch-to-nexus --emit-patch --patch-out /tmp/cp_n2a_smoke → '__SIGNALS_SYNC_PUBLISH__ PASS published=0 patches=0 skipped=0 direction=n6arch-to-nexus' (n6arch->nexus has 0 only-in-n6arch ids — correctly empty). Reverse direction: --cross-publish --direction nexus-to-n6arch --emit-patch → 20 patches generated. Both --batch SIG-X,SIG-Y and --confirm-direction-<dir> flags parse without error.

falsifiers.name: env-gate-NEXUS_SIGNALS_SYNC_PUBLISH-AND-direction-required-for-mutation
falsifiers.criterion: Running --cross-publish with mutation intent must refuse without env=1 AND --direction AND --confirm-direction-<dir>; each layer emits its own raw 66 reason= slug + fix= directive + exit(1)
falsifiers.evidence: Three independent refusal tests, all EXIT=1, zero mutation: (a) hexa run ... --cross-publish --direction nexus-to-n6arch --confirm-direction-nexus-to-n6arch (no env) → '__SIGNALS_SYNC_PUBLISH__ FAIL ... reason=cross-publish-without-env-gate' + 'fix=NEXUS_SIGNALS_SYNC_PUBLISH=1 ... (or add --emit-patch for review-only patches without env-gate)'. (b) hexa run ... --cross-publish (no direction) → 'reason=cross-publish-without-direction' + 'fix=--direction nexus-to-n6arch | n6arch-to-nexus'. (c) NEXUS_SIGNALS_SYNC_PUBLISH=1 hexa run ... --cross-publish --direction nexus-to-n6arch (no confirm) → 'reason=cross-publish-without-direction-confirm' + 'fix=add --confirm-direction-nexus-to-n6arch (explicit non-interactive sign-off)'. Direction REQUIRED even for patch-only mode (so src/target are unambiguous).

falsifiers.name: default-no-mutation-when-flags-absent
falsifiers.criterion: Running with no apply/publish flags must produce ZERO mutation on either real signals file; --cross-publish --emit-patch (no env) also must produce zero mutation
falsifiers.evidence: shasum -a 256 captured before any smoke (pre): nexus=0fcd55e0bd04406d1d4ae220c13dd52719cebf131d652fc0a446ba17961cae91, n6-arch=a5f52d0c4b0f424d878a98076f31ab105ebf28b9352b13bf5bd75568cc8175db. After running ALL of: --self-test, default mode, --by-id, --repos, F1 cross-publish flag tests (both directions), F2 triple refusal tests, F4 patch generation (20 patches), F5 batch filter on both pipelines — final shasum identical to pre. Patch-only --cross-publish --emit-patch path correctly downgrades do_publish=false (via _publish_env_gate_ok() check at pipeline call site) and emits patches without mutation. F6 cross-references this fact.

falsifiers.name: cross-publish-patches-generated-for-20-ids-with-emit-patch
falsifiers.criterion: --cross-publish --direction nexus-to-n6arch --emit-patch must generate exactly 20 .patch files corresponding to the 20 only-in-nexus CROSS_PUBLISH ids; each .patch must be a valid unified diff with proper --- / +++ headers and @@ hunk
falsifiers.evidence: hexa run tool/signals_sync.hexa --cross-publish --direction nexus-to-n6arch --emit-patch --patch-out /tmp/cp_smoke_patches → '__SIGNALS_SYNC_PUBLISH__ PASS published=0 patches=20 skipped=0 direction=nexus-to-n6arch'. ls /tmp/cp_smoke_patches | wc -l = 20. The 20 ids: SIG-BIG-901..907 (7), SIG-BIG-911..917 (7), SIG-CRIT-201, SIG-KURA-201, SIG-META-211, SIG-META-BIG-SUMMARY, SIG-STAR-201, SIG-UNIV-211. Each patch begins '--- n6-arch:atlas.signals.n6:<sig_id> (absent) / +++ n6-arch:atlas.signals.n6:<sig_id> (after-publish, sourced-from nexus)' followed by '@@ -0,0 +1,N @@' hunk and per-line +prefixed @S block content.

falsifiers.name: batch-flag-scopes-to-subset-correctly
falsifiers.criterion: --batch sig1,sig2,... must restrict EITHER --apply-trivial OR --cross-publish to ONLY the listed sig IDs; out-of-batch ids must be [SKIP-BATCH] without action and without errors
falsifiers.evidence: Cross-publish smoke: --cross-publish --direction nexus-to-n6arch --emit-patch --batch SIG-BIG-901,SIG-CRIT-201,SIG-UNIV-211 → __SIGNALS_SYNC_PUBLISH__ PASS published=0 patches=3 skipped=17 (3 patches for the 3 batched ids; 17 [SKIP-BATCH] log lines for the unbatched 17 of 20 candidates). ls /tmp/cp_batch_patches = 3 .patch files, all members of the --batch list. Apply-trivial smoke: --emit-patch --batch SIG-7H-001,SIG-CONS-001 → __SIGNALS_SYNC_APPLY__ PASS applied=0 patches=2 skipped=11 (2 patches for 2 batched APPLY_TRIVIAL ids; 10 of remaining 11 APPLY_TRIVIAL ids skipped + 1 OBSTRUCTION/PROPOSE_ONLY). Default behavior (no --batch) = all-in-class preserved (re-verified --by-id totals: cross_publish=20 apply_trivial=12 unchanged).

falsifiers.name: atlas.signals.n6-byte-identical-in-both-repos-pre-post-smoke
falsifiers.criterion: After ALL F1-F5 smoke tests, /Users/ghost/core/nexus/n6/atlas.signals.n6 AND /Users/ghost/core/canon/atlas/atlas.signals.n6 must be byte-identical (sha verified) to pre-smoke baseline
falsifiers.evidence: shasum -a 256 captured pre (all-tests): nexus=0fcd55e0bd04406d1d4ae220c13dd52719cebf131d652fc0a446ba17961cae91, n6-arch=a5f52d0c4b0f424d878a98076f31ab105ebf28b9352b13bf5bd75568cc8175db. shasum -a 256 captured post (all F1-F5 smokes complete + 23+ patch files written to /tmp): IDENTICAL to pre. `diff /tmp/sigs_pre.sha /tmp/sigs_post_final.sha` empty output. n6-arch repo treated read-only this entire kick; no ./canon file modified. atlas.signals.n6 (L0 in nexus AND in n6-arch) was never written by any code path that ran during smokes.

facts: F1 PASS — --cross-publish, --direction <dir>, --confirm-direction-<dir>, --batch, and the existing --apply-trivial/--emit-patch/--patch-out all coexist; orthogonal-and-composable.

F2 PASS — three-layer refusal enforced: missing direction = reason=cross-publish-without-direction; missing env (mutation intent) = reason=cross-publish-without-env-gate; missing matching confirm = reason=cross-publish-without-direction-confirm. Each is a single-line eprintln raw-66-v2-R1 trailer; exit(1); zero mutation.

F3 PASS — both real signals files (sha 0fcd55…) and (sha a5f52d…) byte-identical pre/post all smokes. Patch-only --cross-publish --emit-patch path correctly produces zero mutation by downgrading do_publish=false at the pipeline call site.

F4 PASS — exactly 20 unified-diff .patch files generated for the 20 only-in-nexus CROSS_PUBLISH ids when --cross-publish --direction nexus-to-n6arch --emit-patch is invoked. IDs match parent #4 witness: SIG-BIG-901..907 (7), SIG-BIG-911..917 (7), SIG-CRIT-201, SIG-KURA-201, SIG-META-211, SIG-META-BIG-SUMMARY, SIG-STAR-201, SIG-UNIV-211.

F5 PASS — --batch SIG-X,SIG-Y filter scopes both --apply-trivial AND --cross-publish to user-listed subset. Empty --batch (default) = all-in-class. Out-of-batch ids tagged [SKIP-BATCH] in pipeline log + counted in skipped total.
F6 PASS — both real signals files (nexus + n6-arch) byte-identical pre/post all smoke runs. Sha verified.

Hard policy honored: real /Users/ghost/core/nexus/n6/atlas.signals.n6 NOT mutated. Real /Users/ghost/core/canon/atlas/atlas.signals.n6 NOT mutated. /Users/ghost/core/canon/* treated read-only this kick.

Honesty: env-gate is genuine. Source-grep confirms NEXUS_SIGNALS_SYNC_PUBLISH is checked at exactly 2 sites (main() entry + per-id defensive recheck inside _run_cross_publish). Both require exact string '1'. Patch-only review path (--cross-publish --emit-patch sans env) is explicitly designed: no backdoor, just an honest 'patches are read-only artifacts' carve-out documented in the help and the gate-block comment.

Honesty: --confirm-direction-<dir> is non-interactive sign-off (matches direction or refuses). Refuses to silently allow non-interactive cross-publish without explicit direction confirmation flag, per kick brief honesty rule.
Cross-publish patches separated from apply-trivial patches: design/patches/signals/cross_publish/ vs design/patches/signals/. Distinct audit trails.

sample_cross_publish_patch_excerpt.content:
--- n6-arch:atlas.signals.n6:SIG-CRIT-201 (absent)
+++ n6-arch:atlas.signals.n6:SIG-CRIT-201 (after-publish, sourced-from nexus)
@@ -0,0 +1,10 @@
+@S SIG-CRIT-201 = critical exponent ↔ n=6 비율 매칭 NULL 우세 :: signal [N6] [PHYS,NULL,META] [MN] [E2]
+  "C2 작업. 5 모형 critical exponent vs n=6 비율 매칭 문헌 검토. ..."
+  refs: [reports/tau_sigma_ratio_universality.md]
+  null_reason: "5 모형 체계적 일치 없음; Vicsek 2D 만 단일 근접"
+  retry_forbidden_until: "2027-04-15"
+  witness: 1
+  discovered_in: n6/session-2026-04-15
+  discovered_at: 2026-04-15T00:00:00Z
+  <- reports/tau_sigma_ratio_universality.md
+

Mirror in the n6arch-to-nexus direction.

Patches serve as audit trail + revert basis.
